signals.headline: Trump says Saudi Arabia's nuclear deal hinges on the kingdom normalizing Israel ties
signals.summary: Trump says Saudi Arabia nuclear deal hinges on Saudi ties with Israel After a Saudi nuclear power deal raised alarm in Israel, President Trump announced online that the arrangement is subject to the kingdom normalizing relations with Israel through the Abraham Accords.
keyTakeaways: With a photograph of himself on the wall as part of "The Presidential Hall of Fame," President Trump gives Crown Prince and Prime Minister Mohammed bin Salman of Saudi Arabia a tour of the White House on Nov.
The Saudi Embassy in Washington did not respond to NPR's request for comment.
It is unclear how the new condition might affect the deal.
signals.whyItMatters: President Trump said on Thursday that a new nuclear energy deal with Saudi Arabia will require the Arab kingdom to normalize relations with Israel.

ja.headline: サウジへの原子力協力、イスラエルとの国交正常化が条件とトランプ氏
ja.summary: サウジアラビアとの原子力エネルギー協定をめぐり、トランプ大統領はオンライン上で声明を発表した。この協定はサウジアラビアがアブラハム合意を通じてイスラエルとの関係を正常化することを条件とする、と述べたものだ。協定の発表はイスラエル側に懸念を生じさせていた。
keyTakeaways: トランプ大統領はホワイトハウスでサウジアラビアのムハンマド・ビン・サルマン皇太子兼首相を案内した
ワシントンのサウジ大使館はコメントの求めに応じていない
この新たな条件が協定にどう影響するかは、現時点では不明
ja.whyItMatters: トランプ大統領は、サウジアラビアとの原子力エネルギー協定にはイスラエルとの国交正常化が必要だと明言した。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw Trump posted something about Saudi Arabia and a nuclear deal?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. He announced that a new nuclear energy deal with Saudi Arabia will require the kingdom to normalize relations with Israel.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So the nuclear deal depends on them making peace with Israel?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: That's what he's saying. The arrangement would be tied to Saudi Arabia joining the Abraham Accords, which are agreements normalizing ties with Israel.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What prompted him to say this now?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: A Saudi nuclear power deal had raised alarm in Israel, so Trump clarified this condition publicly.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Has Saudi Arabia responded to this?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Not yet. The Saudi Embassy in Washington didn't respond to requests for comment.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Do we know if this changes the deal significantly?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It's unclear at this point how the new condition might affect the deal overall.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: アメリカとサウジアラビアが原子力エネルギーの協力で合意しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: この協定には、サウジがイスラエルと国交を結ぶことが条件になります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: トランプ大統領が木曜日、オンラインで発表しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 協定の話が出たとき、イスラエル側には警戒感がありました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: サウジとイスラエルの関係正常化は、中東の外交地図を大きく変える可能性があります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ただ、この条件が実際にどう働くかは、まだはっきりしていません。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: サウジ大使館はコメントを出していません。

signals.headline: India ‘cockroach’ activist Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike as protests spread
signals.summary: The Indian “cockroach” activist Sonam Wangchuk has ended a 26-day hunger strike over an exam paper scandal that has spiralled into a wide-ranging, youth-led wave of demonstrations against Narendra Modi’s government.
keyTakeaways: Wangchuk posted that he had ended the hunger strike in Delhi in the presence of two of Modi’s top ministers after receiving assurances that his conditions would be met by the government.

In a video from his hospital bed, Wangchuk said he had been promised that India’s parliament would take up the issue of accountability in the examination system, which has been accused of failing millions of students.
Gandhi’s longest hunger strike was 21 days.
signals.whyItMatters: The CJP ​movement wants Pradhan to step down over exam paper leaks, which affected 2 million students in May and have been linked to more than a dozen suicides.

ja.headline: インドの活動家ワンチュク氏、26日間の断食を終える
ja.summary: 「ゴキブリ」の異名を持つインドの活動家ソナム・ワンチュク氏が、試験問題漏洩スキャンダルをめぐる26日間の断食を終えた。この問題は若者主導の広範な抗議運動へと発展し、モディ政権への批判が高まっている。
keyTakeaways: モディ首相の閣僚2人の立会いのもと、政府が条件を受け入れると確約を得たとして、ワンチュク氏はデリーでの断食を終えた
病院のベッドから公開した動画で、試験制度の説明責任について議会が審議することを約束されたと語った。数百万人の学生が不正の被害を受けたとされる
ガンジーの最長断食記録は21日間だった
ja.whyItMatters: 試験問題の漏洩は今年5月に200万人の受験生に影響を与え、十数件の自殺との関連も指摘されており、CJP運動はプラダン大臣の辞任を求めている。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw something about an activist in India ending a hunger strike. What happened?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Sonam Wangchuk stopped a 26-day hunger strike in Delhi after meeting with two of Modi's top ministers. He says he got assurances the government would address his concerns.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Twenty-six days is a long time. What was he protesting about?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It started with an exam paper scandal. There were leaks that affected about 2 million students back in May. The protests have grown into a wider youth-led movement against the government.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That's a lot of students. What exactly did he want the government to do?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: From his hospital bed, Wangchuk said he was promised India's parliament would take up accountability in the examination system. The movement also wants a government minister named Pradhan to step down over the leaks.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: This sounds pretty serious for students there.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It is. The exam system has been accused of failing millions of students, and the leaks have been linked to more than a dozen suicides.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: インドで、試験問題の漏洩をめぐる抗議が広がっています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 活動家のソナム・ワンチュク氏は、26日間の断食を続けていました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 政府の閣僚2人が立ち会い、条件を受け入れる約束を得て、断食を終えました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 問題となった試験では、今年5月に200万人の学生が影響を受けました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 不正と関連して、十数件の自殺も報告されています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ワンチュク氏は病院から、議会が試験制度の責任を審議すると伝えました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 若者たちの抗議は、モディ政権への批判へと広がっています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ガンジーの最長断食が21日間だったことを考えると、今回の26日間は異例の長さです。

signals.headline: ‘Forced labour’ rationale for Trump tariffs met with bewilderment
signals.summary: Donald Trump has once again inspired anger and confusion in US allies and trading partners as he imposed a wave of tariffs on more than 80 countries, replacing an expiring global duty introduced earlier this year.
keyTakeaways: The new measures effectively replace blanket 10% tariffs that Trump imposed in February.

They are imposed under section 301 of the US Trade Act of 1974, and the Trump administration has said the new measures are due to the dozens of countries failing to enforce bans on goods produced by forced labour.
The imposition of the new tariffs led to Asian stock markets taking a hammering overnight.
signals.whyItMatters: Kallas said the EU would seek ​clarification from Washington, adding that the bloc had honoured commitments under a transatlantic trade agreement reached in 2025 and viewed ⁠the new tariffs as a shock.

ja.headline: トランプ関税の新たな波、同盟国に困惑広がる
ja.summary: トランプ大統領が80カ国以上を対象とした新たな関税を発動した。今年初めに導入された包括的な関税措置の期限切れに伴う置き換えで、同盟国や貿易相手国の間に怒りと戸惑いが広がっている。
keyTakeaways: 今回の措置は、2月に導入された一律10%関税に実質的に取って代わるもの。
根拠とされたのは1974年通商法第301条で、米政府は強制労働による産品の輸入禁止を履行していないとして各国に課税した。
発動を受け、アジアの株式市場は一夜にして大幅に下落した。
ja.whyItMatters: EUはワシントンに説明を求める方針で、2025年に締結した大西洋横断貿易協定の義務を履行してきたとして、今回の関税を「衝撃」と受け止めている。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I'm seeing headlines about new tariffs from Trump. What's happening now?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Donald Trump just imposed tariffs on more than eighty countries. These replace an earlier ten percent duty that was set to expire.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Wait, what's the reason this time?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The Trump administration says it's because these countries failed to enforce bans on goods made with forced labour. The tariffs are being imposed under a section of the US Trade Act from nineteen seventy-four.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: How are other countries reacting?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: With bewilderment and anger. The EU, for instance, says it honored commitments under a trade agreement reached in twenty twenty-five and views these tariffs as a shock. They're seeking clarification from Washington.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Has this affected markets?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. Asian stock markets took a hammering overnight after the announcement.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So this is basically replacing one set of tariffs with another?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. The new measures effectively replace the blanket ten percent tariffs Trump imposed back in February.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: アメリカが80を超える国に新しい関税をかけました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 今年2月に導入した一律10%の関税が期限を迎えたため、それに代わる措置です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 根拠として挙げられたのは、強制労働でつくられた製品の輸入を各国が十分に取り締まっていないという理由でした。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: アメリカは1974年の通商法を使い、この措置を正当化しています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ただ、EUをはじめ多くの国は困惑を隠せません。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 去年結んだ大西洋の貿易協定を守ってきたのに、なぜ今なのかという疑問が広がっています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 発表を受けて、アジアの株式市場は一夜で大きく値を下げました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 貿易の先行きが見えにくくなると、市場はすぐに反応します。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 同盟国との信頼が揺らぐ中、世界経済の不透明感が増しています。

signals.headline: Tesla’s robotaxi promises are clashing with reality
signals.summary: In an earnings call yesterday, Tesla CEO Elon Musk did his best to paint a positive portrait of the company’s robotaxi program.
keyTakeaways: New cities are being added, more miles are being driven, and more people are experiencing Tesla’s unsupervised vehicles.
Tesla did its best to frame the situation as headed in the right direction.
Ashok Elluswamy, the company’s head of AI, said that Tesla’s unsupervised robotaxis have driven 380,000 miles across six cities in two different states.
signals.whyItMatters: A more restrictive regulatory environment could limit the total addressable market for Tesla robotaxis, which would clash with the company’s message about a more generalized service that can drive anywhere, under any conditions.

ja.headline: テスラの自動運転タクシー、理想と現実の間で
ja.summary: テスラのイーロン・マスクCEOは昨日の決算説明会で、同社のロボタクシー事業について前向きな見通しを語った。
keyTakeaways: 対応都市の拡大、走行距離の増加、無人車両の体験者も着実に増えている。
テスラは事業が正しい方向へ進んでいると強調した。
AI責任者のアショク・エルスワミー氏によれば、無人ロボタクシーは2州6都市で計約38万マイルを走行した。
ja.whyItMatters: 規制環境が厳しくなれば、場所や条件を問わず走れるという同社の描く構想と、実際に展開できる市場の広がりとの間に、大きな齟齬が生じかねない。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So Tesla had an earnings call yesterday about their robotaxi program?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. CEO Elon Musk tried to paint an optimistic picture of where things stand.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What did they say exactly?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They're adding new cities, driving more miles, and getting more people to try the unsupervised vehicles. Their AI head said the robotaxis have driven 380,000 miles across six cities in two states.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That sounds like progress. So what's the clash with reality?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Well, regulations could end up being more restrictive, which would limit where these robotaxis can actually operate.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And that's a problem for Tesla because...?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Because their whole message is about a service that can drive anywhere, under any conditions. If regulations box them in, that vision doesn't really hold up.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: テスラのイーロン・マスクは昨日、自動運転タクシーの進み具合について前向きな話をしました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 対応する街が増え、走る距離も伸び、実際に乗る人も増えているといいます。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: AI部門の責任者によれば、無人の車はこれまでに2つの州の6つの街で約38万マイルを走ったそうです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: テスラは順調だと言いたいようですが、規制の壁は思ったより高いかもしれません。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: どこでもどんな条件でも走れるという構想と、実際に許される場所との間には、まだ大きな隔たりがあります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 市場の広がりが限られれば、会社が描く将来像にも影を落とすことになります。

signals.headline: Geekbench 7 will push your computer or phone even harder for better benchmarking
signals.summary: It looks and acts like Geekbench 6, but under the hood are larger datasets, new audio / video tests, and more tweaks.
keyTakeaways: Primate Labs is releasing Geekbench 7, the latest generation of its popular benchmarking tool.
Geekbench 7 features new video and audio encoding / decoding tests, a redesigned multi-core test, and larger, more demanding datasets for CPUs and GPUs to crunch through.
It otherwise looks and acts just like Geekbench 6, and a Pro license will cost the same $99 (discounted to $79 until August 6th).
signals.whyItMatters: I’ve always liked how simple and fast Geekbench is to run, but its short duration also means it’s a better test of quick bursts of computing rather than sustained loads that can throttle some CPUs and GPUs.

ja.headline: ベンチマークツール「Geekbench 7」登場——より高負荷な測定へ
ja.summary: Primate Labsが「Geekbench 7」を公開した。見た目や操作感はGeekbench 6とほぼ変わらないが、内部ではデータセットが大型化し、音声・映像のエンコード／デコードテストが新たに加わるなど、測定の精度と負荷が引き上げられている。
keyTakeaways: 人気ベンチマークツールの最新世代、Geekbench 7がリリース。
映像・音声の符号化テストを新設し、マルチコアテストを刷新。CPUとGPUに課すデータセットも大幅に拡張された。
外観や操作性はGeekbench 6を踏襲。Proライセンスは99ドルで、8月6日までは79ドルに割引される。
ja.whyItMatters: 手軽に素早く実行できる点がGeekbenchの魅力だが、テスト時間が短いぶん、持続的な高負荷よりも瞬発的な処理能力を測るツールとして捉えておくのが適切だ。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So there's a new version of Geekbench coming out?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, Geekbench 7. It's the latest version of that popular benchmarking tool people use to test how fast their computers and phones are.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What's actually different about it?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Under the hood, it's got larger datasets and some new tests — specifically for video and audio encoding and decoding. They also redesigned how it tests multi-core performance.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Does it look totally different to use?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: No, it looks and acts just like Geekbench 6. So if you've used it before, it'll feel familiar.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And I'm guessing it's harder on your device now?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: That's the idea — bigger, more demanding workloads for both CPUs and GPUs. Though it's still designed to run quickly, so it's really testing short bursts rather than how a device handles long, sustained loads.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What does it cost?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: A Pro license is ninety-nine dollars, but they're offering it for seventy-nine until August sixth.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: パソコンやスマートフォンの性能を測る定番ツール、Geekbenchの新版が出ました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: Primate Labsが公開したGeekbench 7は、見た目も使い方も前の版とほぼ同じです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ただし内部では、処理するデータの量が大きく増え、映像や音声の変換テストも新しく追加されています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: マルチコアの測定方法も見直され、CPUとGPUにかかる負担は以前より重くなりました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: Geekbenchは短時間で結果が出るため、瞬間的な処理の速さを知るには便利です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 一方で、長く動かしたときの発熱や性能低下までは捉えにくい面もあります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: Pro版の価格は99ドルで、8月6日までは79ドルで購入できます。
